agency: New York City Department of Correction (DOC)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/doc (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) and the Inmate Lookup Service linked from the site, identified as the 'P.I.C. Lookup' at a073-ils-web.nyc.gov/inmatelookup — a legacy Apache MyFaces (JavaServer Faces) web application (javax.faces.ViewState, myfaces markers, WebSphere-style JSESSIONID, SameSite=Strict) with no API. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Correction (DOC)' via the Socrata Discovery API (q=inmate/jail/correction) and pulled all 15 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' (Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM) informational site + a legacy Apache MyFaces (JSF/Java) Inmate Lookup Service ('P.I.C. Lookup', a073-ils-web.nyc.gov) with no API; Socrata SODA open data
meta.netnew: VisitScheduling — schedule a visit with a person in custody (no API today; arranged by phone, in person, or vendor portal). Complaint / records request is a second net-new write.
meta.tagline: DOC over-publishes accountability data and even runs a live custody lookup, yet the lookup is a JSF screen with no API and the human transactions — visiting someone, filing a complaint — have no digital contract at all.
meta.headline: A transparency-heavy, transaction-poor domain: 15 open datasets and a live P.I.C. lookup, but the lookup is a legacy Java screen with no API and scheduling a visit has no surface.

scorecard_notes.platform: Standard NYC.gov Livesite chassis plus a legacy Apache MyFaces (JSF) lookup on a WebSphere-era stack
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or site API; the informational site is HTML only
scorecard_notes.openData: 15 Socrata datasets — unusually rich accountability data (custody, admissions, deaths, incidents, security indicators)
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core person-in-custody data is published daily and searchable live, but only via a JSF screen and daily snapshots — no per-person API
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — visits, complaints, and records requests are phone/mail/in-person or vendor portals
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; the live lookup is a JavaScript/JSF app hostile to automation
scorecard_notes.ownership: First-party datasets and a city-hosted lookup (a073.nyc.gov), not a rented SaaS — reasonably owned, but the lookup is undocumented legacy

headline_findings: DOC is transparency-heavy but transaction-poor: 15 NYC Open Data datasets publish the daily in-custody population, admissions/discharges, deaths, slashings/stabbings, fights, assaults on staff, staff injuries, emergency lock-ins, and Local Law 33/85 security and visitation indicators.

It also runs a LIVE real-time lookup — the Inmate Lookup Service / 'P.I.C. Lookup' at a073-ils-web.nyc.gov/inmatelookup — but that lookup is a legacy Apache MyFaces (JavaServer Faces) web application with no API, no OpenAPI, and no JSON; it is reachable only by a human in a browser.

The two things the public actually needs to DO — schedule a visit with a person in custody, and file a complaint or records/FOIL request — have no machine-readable surface at all; they are handled by phone, in person, mail, or vendor/OpenRecords portals.

Individual-level custody data exists in two disconnected forms: a daily anonymized snapshot on Open Data (INMATEID only) and a live named lookup keyed on NYSID / Book & Case number — neither is an API.
Net-new write surface: schedule a visit (VisitScheduling); a second net-new write is file a complaint / records request. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 15 DOC datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DOC API — accountability/statistics data only, no live lookup.
Real-time person-in-custody search by NYSID / Book & Case / name; server-rendered JavaServer Faces (javax.faces.ViewState, myfaces), WebSphere-style JSESSIONID, JavaScript-only, no JSON/OpenAPI.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
FOIL / records requests routed here; no DOC-specific API.

fruit.name: Daily Inmates In Custody
fruit.notes: The most-viewed DOC dataset. Anonymized per-person snapshot keyed on INMATEID with age/race/gender, custody level, top charge, SRG/BRADH flags, admit/discharge dates.

fruit.name: Inmate Lookup Service ('P.I.C. Lookup')
fruit.notes: The live, named person-in-custody lookup keyed on NYSID / Book & Case / name. A legacy Apache MyFaces (JSF) web app — no API, no JSON, JavaScript-only. The obvious thing to front with an owned API.

fruit.name: DOC Facilities (jails)
fruit.notes: No facility-roster dataset exists; the FACILITY dimension (AMKC, GRVC, RNDC, RMSC, OBCC, VCBC, EMTC, WF…) recurs across incident/lock-in datasets. A derived reference entity, mostly Rikers Island.

fruit.name: Safety & Security Incidents
fruit.notes: Six parallel incident datasets keyed on INCIDENT_ID, per facility, with reported/incident dates and reasons. Strong accountability corpus; unified into one IncidentReport model.

fruit.name: Daily Population & Security Indicators
fruit.notes: Rate-per-100-ADP security indicators, average daily population, the Article 730 transfer waitlist, and medical non-production — the time-series accountability metrics.

fruit.name: Schedule a visit
opendata_match: Local Law 85 – Visitation Quarterly b3eu-nmy6 (aggregate only)
fruit.notes: Family/legal visits are arranged by phone, in person, or vendor registration/tele-visit systems; only aggregate visitation counts (LL85) are published. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain — VisitScheduling.

fruit.name: File a complaint / records request
fruit.notes: Complaints, grievances, and FOIL/records requests are filed by phone, mail, in person, or the citywide OpenRecords portal — no DOC API, no status contract. A second net-new WRITE surface.

fruit.name: Hart Island Burial Records
opendata_match: DOC Hart Island Burial Records f5mc-f3zp (5c)
fruit.notes: DOC-administered public burial records (name, age, death date, place of death). A distinctive DOC dataset; adjacent to the custody domain.

fruit.name: Aggregate Employee Statistics
fruit.notes: Uniformed/civilian staffing rollups (rank title, gender, leave status). Aggregate workforce accountability; folded under the indicators family.
